sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.7g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, the whole appeal is cheap, filling nutritional payoff.
sections.subtitleText: 0.7g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, the whole appeal is cheap, filling nutritional payoff.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 63.4g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 63.4g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 21.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 21.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet.
sections.subtitleText: outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 32% daily value, iron at 25% daily value, potassium at 27% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 32% daily value, iron at 25% daily value, potassium at 27% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: filling for the calories. legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety. does a lot of jobs at once. protein, fibre, and meal utility are why legumes stay valuable. fibre-forward category identity. legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: protein quality still has a ceiling. good, but not meat or dairy level. can take planning or prep. that is a small but real friction cost. prep time can still be a barrier. dry legumes especially ask more from the cook.

closing.summary: Lima Beans has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 64 from a base nutrition score of 64. Context barely changed the result.
